summary: be lazier about holding on to connections
description:
Imagine a long running workload that has 10 threads. Each thread has a client they are using to communicate with the MongoDB server. 5 are doing reads, 5 are doing writes.

If the replicaSet is a 3 member node, we will hold onto 30 connections even tho only 10 can be used at a time.

We can improve this a bit.

summary: Improve error messages for connection failures
description: As discussed in CDRIVER-435 and CDRIVER-432, we should improve the driver's error messages for connection failures - i.e. timeout vs OS running out of sockets, etc.

summary: Add support for storage options to index creation helper
description: Per DRIVERS-194

summary: Add support for storage options to collection creation helper
description: Per DRIVERS-195

summary: "Failure to buffer 4 bytes" error on OS X with 300 client threads
mongoc_collection_find does not report dropped connection
description: If client drops connection to the server, mongoc_collection_find() returns empty cursor as if no documents match the query, rather than signalling an error.  Logs show "WARNING:  stream: Failure to buffer 4 bytes: Failed to buffer 4 bytes within 300000 milliseconds," but this is not passed up to the application level.

summary: MONGOC_QUERY_OPLOG_REPLY should be MONGOC_QUERY_OPLOG_REPLAY
description:
When implementing the HHVM prototype, I noticed that the MONGOC_QUERY_OPLOG_REPLY constant is misspelled. It should be MONGOC_QUERY_OPLOG_REPLAY as that's how it's defined at http://docs.mongodb.org/meta-driver/latest/legacy/mongodb-wire-protocol/#op-query

Luckily this is an internal flag only.

summary: Add "How to Ask for Help" section to Github readme
Add make check to build
description: make check should be executed as a separate task after compilation.

summary: mongoc_collection_find and logical $and/$or
description:
When construction a query using BCON_NEW, using the $and or $or operator will fail if there is another expression prior to the use of $and/$or.

The following query will yield results:
query = BCON_NEW("$and","[","{",GROUPSTART,"{","$lte",BCON_INT32(addr),"}","}","{",GROUPSTOP,"{","$gte",BCON_INT32(addr),"}","}","]");

The following will NOT yield results:
 query = BCON_NEW(GROUPAUTO, BCON_BOOL(true),
                     "$and","[","{",GROUPSTART,"{","$lte",BCON_INT32(addr),"}","}","{",GROUPSTOP,"{","$gte",BCON_INT32(addr),"}","}","]");

If I call a bson_as_json on the second query, and use that json to create a raw query from the shell. It WILL yield results.

summary: mongoc_cursor_clone() ignores fields
Use provided username for X509 authentication
description:
The intention is that users explicitly provide the username for X509 auth.
Currently the driver ignores it, and pulls it out from the provided pem file, which has to be set via mongoc_client_set_ssl_opts().

phongo however doesn't use the mongoc ssl wrapper, and therefore never calls that function.

mongoc needs to first check if a username was provided, and then fallback on extracting the subject from the cert.

summary: Get rid of non-prefixed macros in libbson and libmongoc
description:
libbson provides MAX, MIN and ABS macros.   We really shouldn't as these occasionally collide with system provided defaults that are sometimes defined unconditionally.

Either way, it'll be better to namespace them and get away from bare macros

summary: UNIX sockets do not work
description:
When trying to connect to a UNIX socket I always get this error:

 "Failed to connect to UNIX domain socket" error.

Also, the documentation is wrong, the socket is created at: /tmp/mongodb-27017.sock and not /tmp/mongodb.sock

summary: mongoc never reports WriteConcern error
description: mongoc never reports WriteConcernError from the 2.6+ write commands

summary: 1.1.0 post release bump breaks --with-libbson=system
description: This commit https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/ed4980d00bdaf2957cc41b80c087589aabee6147 bumps the minimum libbson version to 1.1.1, which doesn't exist yet. This means it will always build with bundled libbson, which breaks packaging the libraries separately.

summary: Connection error only with 2.8 and WT Engne
description:
Odd one this - I get the following error.

2015/01/08 14:03:06.0488: [18646]:  WARNING:       client: Failed to connect to: ipv4 10.14.0.198:27017, error: 115, Operation now in progress

When trying to connect to MongoDB.

C Client, Already have 1024 open connection, briefly opening another 1024

Plenty of file handles - doesn't happen with MMAP engine (I know that makes no sense)

summary: Failed to read 4 bytes from socket
description:
The error "Failed to read 4 bytes from socket" seems to be the "go to error" when something happens to the socket.
This for example is easily reproducible with a failed X509 authentication failure.

It would be nice if the error message could be more userfriendly.

summary: 2 minor errors during visual studio 2008 build and link
description:
Successfully pass VS 2008 compilation with 2 minor change in source code :

1/
at mongo-c-driver-1.1.0\src\mongoc\mongoc-database.c, l. 688 :
variable 'bson_string_t *buf' shall be defined at the begin of _mongoc_database_get_collection_info_legacy function since we compile C only code.

2/
at mongo-c-driver-1.1.0\build\cmake\libmongoc.def, l.144 to l.146 :
mongoc_rand_add,
mongoc_rand_seed and
mongoc_rand_status
symbols shall be removed (since no related C symbols seems to be found)
Keeping this symbol prevent link phase to succeed.

summary: failed client creation doesn't result in Null returned
description:
Utilising the mongoc driver, ive discovered that when you create a connection, using
client = mongoc_client_new(connection_string);

The returned value when the mongodb isnt running is not a null value. This means any tests/checks carried out do not catch that the server hasnt been connected to, causing issues further down the line. 

Looking in the source I can't see anything that would set a value Null, either in v1.0.2, not v1.1.0 that was recently released.

summary: mongoc_uri should normalize hostnames to lower case
description: The SDAM spec [mandates that hostnames be normalized to lower case|http://emptysqua.re/server-discovery-and-monitoring.html#hostnames-are-normalized-to-lower-case].  This includes hostnames passed in via the initial seed list.

summary: C driver does not support IPv6 addresses
vectorized writes are pathological for TLS using BIO_write
description:
The mongoc_stream_writev api is somewhat pathological for TLS streams at this point, because we make multiple calls into BIO_write, which adds overhead in the driver, over the wire and in the server.

We should coalesce small writes to avoid this penalty

summary: Memory leak in mongoc_client_get_database_names function
description:
Memory leak in mongoc_client_get_database_names function. 

mongoc_cursor_t *cursor is nor freed after use. Should be added:
in file mongoc-client.c (after 1322 line)

mongoc_cursor_destroy (cursor);

summary: Received invalid SCRAM reply from MongoDB server
description:
The error message "Received invalid SCRAM reply from MongoDB server" really doesn't mean anything to a normal user.

I'd suggest to use the actual error message from MongoDB - which makes whole lot more sense (albeit, still inaccurate): "Authentication failed."

In reality, the username didn't exist - but that is an implementation detail we shouldn't expose to the user: SERVER-15295

summary: Can't compile with MinGW-w64
description:
I can't compile mongo-c-driver 1.1.0 with MinGW w64.

Steps to reproduce :

1. Open a MSYS shell
2. Set the environment (for OpenSSL support)

export CFLAGS="-IC:/Libs/amd64/libopenssl/include"
export LDFLAGS="-LC:/Libs/amd64/libopenssl/lib"

3. Run configure and make

./configure --prefix=C:/Libs/amd64/libmongo
make

The error:

Making all in src/libbson
make[1]: Entering directory `/c/mongo-c-driver-1.1.0/src/libbson'
make[1]: Nothing to be done for `all'.
make[1]: Leaving directory `/c/mongo-c-driver-1.1.0/src/libbson'
make[1]: Entering directory `/c/mongo-c-driver-1.1.0'
  CCLD     libmongoc-priv.la
libtool:   error: can't build i686-pc-mingw32 shared library unless -no-undefined is specified
make[1]: *** [libmongoc-priv.la] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory `/c/mongo-c-driver-1.1.0'
make: *** [all-recursive] Error 1

summary: Fix node management in replica set cluster reconnect
description:
Management of cluster nodes was badly broken by our fix for CDRIVER-453.

Basically, you always talk to the number of nodes in your original uri list, rather than expanding and contracting based on your peer list derived from hosts in the ismaster call.

summary: connectTimeoutMS (and probably others) option ignored unless in lower case
sdam/ss tests don't integrate with the rest of the tests
description:
The SS/SDAM tests we have now write their output in a non json format and don't work with the rest of the framework (they can't be individually run with -l)

We should update the tests to use something like TestSuite_Add to include the individual test cases.

summary: Collection-level update and bulk functions do not inherit write concern
description: It looks like write concern inheritance was originally added in [this commit|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/a735be1e4c296aa753dd3e761b1e113be0362625]; however, it was removed from {{mongoc_collection_update()}} at some point. It appears it was never added to {{mongoc_collection_create_bulk_operation()}}.

summary: Segfault during OpenSSL cleanup routines
description:
When more then one libraries are using OpenSSL, mongoc will cause OpenSSL destruction routines to segfault due to not resetting the callback retrieving the thread id.

Furthermore, we need to check if there has been callback registered already before trying to register ours - and during cleanup, we should make sure its our callback we are cleaning.

summary: Executing bulk operation without write concern should inherit from client
don't pass server descriptions around unnecessarily
description: In the cluster code, some methods take server descriptions as parameters when really, we shouldn't be passing those objects down so deep.  The information those functions need should be pulled out of the server description or cluster node at a higher level and then passed down.  Passing server descriptions around will encourage us to use them in the future in ways that may cause race conditions.

summary: select-related methods in cluster don't need write_concern
description: Don't pass them write_concern unnecessarily.

summary: mallard2man.py should only use Python standard lib
description: The Python script in libmongoc and libbson repos takes our ".page" XML files and creates man pages. It should use the Python standard library, not lxml, and should be compatible with all Python 2.6+ to ease contributors' lives.

summary: write command code shouldn't use server descriptions
description: In the write command code, we should not rely on server descriptions for any connection-related information.  Instead, we should ask the cluster for wire version information, which will have been stored from the last ismaster run on that stream.

summary: URI parsing does not validate out-of-range port numbers
URI parsing should not assume write concern defaults
description: I noticed that {{(_mongoc_uri_build_write_concern()}} parses {{w=1}} as {{MONGOC_WRITE_CONCERN_W_DEFAULT}}. Combined with CDRIVER-580, this means the driver could send an empty write concern to the server, which could result in an unexpected [server-side default|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/command/replSetGetConfig/#replSetGetConfig.settings.getLastErrorDefaults] being applied.

summary: deprecate mongoc_client_get_max_message_size() and mongoc_client_get_max_bson_size()
description: Deprecate and eliminate all internal uses.  Max msg size and max bson obj size are values that vary per server, and should be used in the context of a single server, not the cluster as a whole.

summary: SDAM code should normalize hostnames to lowercase
Indirect loss' in SDAM
description:

summary: Clients don't reconnect after primary step down
description:
mongoc_cluster_t's have a try_sendv and a sendv method to actually write bytes for commands.  For some reason we use try_sendv after the cluster is born, so we don't reconnect if select fails.

That doesn't make any sense.  We should just use regular cluster_sendv() in all cases.

summary: v1.2 no longer uses stream initiator callback
description:
This breaks drivers built on top mongoc.

There is also a TODO in the code for this:

src/mongoc/mongoc-topology-scanner.c:   /* TODO hook up a callback for this so you can use your own stream initiator */

summary: Bulk Writes sent to wrong server in write command mode
description:
The bulk->hint is used to do all the precondition checks, including wire version, to decide if we should do legacy op inserts or write commands.

When legacy op inserts are detect we submit the write to the correct bulk->hint server.

However, in the case of write commands this hint gets lost mid-way and a new server selected.

This has two consequences:
 - We could wind up attempting bulk write on a 2.4 server
 - A bulk write to a secondary `local` database will be re-routed to the primary, effectively resulting in dataloss.

summary: ssl no longer supported
description:
SSL connections don't seem to be supported anymore.
This might be artifact of CDRIVER-577

summary: Latest tar has broken link in Github
description:
On this page https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver
it points to the latest tar saying, 
"The most current release is 1.1.5 which you can download here. " and points to here: 
https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/releases/download/1.1.5/mongo-c-driver-1.1.5.tar.gz

but it does not exist, i.e. a broken link

summary: Need to add mongoc-client-pool-private.h in mongoc-client-pool-private.c
description:
the function mongoc_client_pool_get_size() is declared in mongoc-client-pool-private.h and its implementation in mongoc-client-pool-private.c.

The issue is mongoc-client-pool-private.c doesn't include mongoc-client-pool-private.h, therefore, the compiler doesn't see the declaration of this function while compiling the implementation of this function. There is a warning on OS X.

summary: Documentation bug
description: The manual page for mongoc_collection_find_indexes instead has the function  mongoc_database_find_collections in the synopsis. This is very confusing: http://api.mongodb.org/c/current/mongoc_collection_find_indexes.html

summary: Documentation errors
description: On the GitHub project page  (https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver) the documentation is talking about a version 1.1.1 but it does not exist. The most recent version found on GitHub is 1.1.0
